steps.title: Match sensitivity to information
steps.content: The second team's hypothesis links sensor sensitivity to the locations where pressure differences provide the most information. The earlier study gives a clear pattern: fish with narrower heads should have that highest-information point closer to the snout, while fish with wider heads should have it farther from the snout. A supporting finding must show the most sensitive sensors following that same narrow-head versus wide-head pattern.

steps.title: Compare the species pattern
steps.content: A says the narrower longnose gar has its most sensitive sensors closer to the snout than the wider devil catfish does, matching the predicted locations. B puts both species' most sensitive sensors near the snout, so it loses the width-based difference. C supports the gar pattern but says the devil catfish has no sensitivity peak. D reverses the expected relationship by placing the wider species' most sensitive sensors closer to the snout. The answer is A.
